rows.locks: Godot adapter IR becomes a deterministic logic touchpoint hand-off artifact with coupling classifications, oracle requirements, Era R tasks, and an honest fidelity report without claiming any behavior-bearing unit is ported.

Lossy imports, unsupported features, script refs, input signals, physics/collision signals, rendering/VFX gaps, and behavior-bearing units remain visible Red gaps and cannot be laundered into a clean Green report.

The logic hand-off validator rejects claimed_ported_units and oracle-missing records with port permission, so ungated or auto-translated port claims fail until later Ouroforge-native oracle evidence passes.
The hand-off artifact state-hash is stable for identical canonical logic inventories, changes when source data changes, and validation rejects stale hashes after artifact tampering.

Coverage v80 records the one-way on-ramp, source-project/open-text legal boundary, clean-room Era R re-derivation hand-off, Rust data-plane ownership, no Studio trusted writes, no decompiled source copying, and #1/#23 open governance anchors.

boundary: Scenario Coverage v80 is a Rust data-plane regression suite for Era O M91 logic touchpoint detection and re-derivation hand-off. It locks source-project/open-text adapter IR inventory into clean-room Era R tasks, no foreign runtime bridge, no auto-port or ported claim without a passing oracle, honest Red fidelity gaps for lossy imports, deterministic state-hash behavior including stale-hash rejection, and Rust-owned artifact truth. Elixir/Phoenix Studio is not touched and has no trusted write or artifact semantics authority. #1 and #23 remain open.
